title: ceropegia piersii
intro: Ceropegia piersii, also known as the 'Pier's Ceropegia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Ceropegia piersii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water to create a medicinal preparation.
